context.project_description: Implement token usage tracking for LLM queries by integrating with existing instrumentation infrastructure to log input/output token counts for chat and embedding operations.
key_requirements: Track input and output token counts for chat and embed calls
Use existing InstrumentedProxy and BenchmarkHook mechanisms
Log token usage per method call with structured logging
constraints: Do not introduce new abstractions like TokenCounter class
Only instrument methods in ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider protocols
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py contains InstrumentedProxy and BenchmarkHook protocols; fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py defines ChatProvider and EmbeddingProvider protocols with required methods
stakeholders: engineering team maintaining LLM provider interfaces
operations team monitoring cost and usage

in_scope: tracking input token counts for chat and embed methods
tracking output token counts where available for chat and embed
logging token usage per method call
out_of_scope: adding generic observability layer beyond token counting

existing_files: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Cohere provider wrappers using the official SDK.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls.
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/token_hook.py — A hook that counts and logs token usage for LLM operations.
fitz_ai/llm/factory.py — Updated to register the TokenTrackingHook with relevant LLM providers.

assumptions.assumption: The proposed solution introduces a new TokenCounter class and integrates it into provider creation via wrap_with_token_tracking, extending beyond the original scope of tracking only token usage for LLM queries
assumptions.impact: This could lead to unnecessary complexity, potential conflicts with existing instrumentation system, and deviation from the stated goal of minimal changes. It may also introduce redundant abstractions that complicate maintenance.

assumptions.assumption: There exists a file `fitz_ai/llm/providers/token_counter.py` which does not actually exist in the provided codebase
assumptions.impact: Including references to non-existent files would cause build failures, confusion during implementation, and wasted effort on developers trying to locate missing components.

assumptions.assumption: The `InstrumentedProxy` class already exists in `fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py` and can be leveraged for wrapping LLM provider methods without requiring custom wrapper logic
assumptions.impact: If this assumption is incorrect, the solution must reinvent instrumentation mechanisms or risk implementing a suboptimal approach that doesn't integrate well with existing systems.

assumptions.assumption: The term 'instrumentation' refers to a specific mechanism defined in `fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py` and is sufficient for implementation without further clarification
assumptions.impact: Ambiguity around what exactly constitutes 'instrumentation' might result in misalignment between the intended solution and actual execution, leading to incorrect wrapping or logging behavior.

assumptions.assumption: Only certain methods (e.g., `chat`, `embed`) should track tokens, excluding streaming, vision, reranking operations
assumptions.impact: Misunderstanding the scope could result in either too much overhead from tracking irrelevant calls or missing critical token usage data for important operations like chat and embeddings.

assumptions.assumption: OpenAI SDK provides structured responses with usage fields such as 'prompt_tokens' and 'completion_tokens', which are available across all LLM providers
assumptions.impact: If other LLM providers (e.g., Anthropic, Cohere) do not follow this exact structure, the fallback logic will be needed to handle missing or differently formatted token data.

approaches.name: Wrapper-Based Instrumentation
approaches.description: Create proxy wrappers around LLM providers that capture token usage during calls without modifying core protocols. Uses existing instrumentation system to track input/output lengths and aggregate across sessions.
pros: Maintains backward compatibility with all existing provider interfaces
Leverages existing instrumentation infrastructure in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py
Can be enabled/disabled via configuration without code changes
Provides detailed per-call tracking for debugging and observability
cons: Requires wrapping each provider instance, adding complexity to client factory
Token counting is approximate when estimating from text length rather than actual tokenization
Must maintain thread-local or session-scoped storage for aggregated usage
Doesn't integrate with providers that expose real token counts (like OpenAI's response metadata)
best_for: Systems where modifying core protocols is not feasible due to breaking changes

approaches.name: Protocol Extension with Metadata Return Types
approaches.description: Modify LLM provider protocols (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider) to return structured results that include token usage metadata alongside responses. This requires updating all provider implementations.
pros: Provides accurate token tracking by leveraging providers' native token counting capabilities
Clear API contract for consumers expecting usage data
Enables precise cost estimation and budgeting
Centralizes token handling logic in provider layer
cons: Breaking change to existing provider interfaces that may affect all clients
Requires modification of all current LLM implementations (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama)
Adds complexity to each provider's implementation with metadata handling
May not be immediately backward compatible with third-party integrations
best_for: Systems where token tracking is critical and can tolerate breaking changes for improved accuracy

architecture.reasoning: The 'Protocol Extension with Metadata Return Types' approach is technically ideal but infeasible given the existing architecture - all provider protocols return raw strings without metadata (e.g., ChatProvider.chat returns str, not structured data). The contract sheet explicitly states that TokenUsageTracker class and related interfaces don't exist in codebase. Furthermore, modifying core protocols would be a breaking change to existing implementations like OpenAIChat.chat(), AnthropicChat.chat(), etc., which currently return only strings. The Wrapper-Based Instrumentation approach is the pragmatic solution because it can leverage the existing BenchmarkHook infrastructure from fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py without breaking contracts, even though it requires approximate token estimation rather than precise counting.

key_tradeoffs.protocol_compatibility_vs_feature_enrich: Extending LLM provider protocols to include token usage metadata would break backward compatibility with existing implementations, requiring either major refactoring or wrapper-based approaches.
key_tradeoffs.precision_vs_efficiency_in_tracking: Accurate token counting requires integrating actual tokenizers (like tiktoken) which adds computational overhead, versus estimating from text length which is faster but less precise.
key_tradeoffs.centralized_vs_decentralized_aggregation: Centralized token tracking through a registry service provides consistent aggregation but creates single points of failure and complexity, while decentralized tracking at each call site is more robust but harder to maintain consistently.

technology_considerations: OpenAI SDK integration with usage metadata extraction for accurate token counting
Ollama API response parsing to capture prompt_eval_count and eval_count fields
Anthropic SDK usage field access for input_tokens and output_tokens tracking
Cohere SDK token_count object utilization for comprehensive token metrics
Instrumentation hook extension for capturing structured metadata during LLM calls
Thread-local storage implementation for per-request token state management
Decorator-based wrapper approach to maintain protocol compatibility while adding token tracking
Context manager pattern for automatic token tracking around LLM invocations
architecture.scope_statement: Implement a wrapper-based instrumentation system that tracks LLM token usage across all provider calls by estimating input/output lengths and aggregating per-session totals, using the existing BenchmarkHook framework in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py.

adrs.title: ADR: Extend LLM Provider Protocols with Token Usage Metadata
adrs.context: The current LLM provider protocols (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, etc.) do not support returning token usage information, making it impossible to track and aggregate token consumption across LLM calls. This prevents users from understanding the cost of queries in terms of LLM tokens.
adrs.decision: Modify core LLM provider protocols to return structured response objects that include both content and token usage metadata, rather than changing method signatures extensively or requiring wrapper approaches.
adrs.rationale: This approach follows the existing architectural patterns (protocol-based abstraction) while providing a clean mechanism for token tracking. It enables precise token accounting at each provider level without breaking backward compatibility through careful design of new return types that can be gradually adopted.
consequences: Requires updating all LLM provider implementations to return structured responses
Creates a new contract that must be implemented by all providers going forward
Enables accurate per-call token tracking and aggregation across query sessions
Provides detailed insights into which components consume the most tokens
alternatives_considered: Wrapper-based approach - rejected because it would require wrapping every provider call manually and could break existing code paths without careful coordination
Instrumentation hook extension - rejected because current BenchmarkHook protocol doesn't support returning structured metadata that can carry token usage information from the call start to end
Decorator pattern around LLM calls - rejected because estimation-based token counting is imprecise and requires integration with actual tokenizer libraries, making it unreliable for production use

adrs.title: ADR: Implement Centralized Token Usage Aggregation in Query Engine
adrs.context: The FitzKragEngine currently has no mechanism to track or aggregate LLM token usage across multiple calls during a single query processing session. This makes it impossible to provide meaningful cost information back to users.
adrs.decision: Modify the FitzKragEngine.answer() method to accumulate and store token usage data from all provider calls, then include this aggregated information in final Answer objects.
adrs.rationale: This ensures that each user-facing query response includes comprehensive token usage metrics. It maintains consistency with how other metadata is handled within the engine and provides end-users with actionable cost insights for their queries.
consequences: FitzKragEngine will need to be modified to track token data from all LLM interactions
Answer objects must be extended or replaced to carry aggregated usage information
Performance overhead of tracking during query processing
Requires coordination between providers and engine components for consistent data flow
alternatives_considered: API layer aggregation - rejected because token data is not available at the API boundary; it's lost in provider calls before reaching that point
Thread-local storage approach - rejected because there's no existing state management infrastructure and this would create hidden dependencies
Separate tracking service - rejected because it adds complexity without clear benefits over direct aggregation within engine

adrs.title: ADR: Integrate Token Usage Tracking with Existing Instrumentation System
adrs.context: The existing instrumentation system in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py provides a framework for benchmarking and monitoring but does not currently support capturing or aggregating token usage data.
adrs.decision: Enhance the BenchmarkHook protocol to include methods specifically designed for tracking token usage, allowing token counting to be performed during LLM calls while maintaining compatibility with existing instrumentation behavior.
adrs.rationale: This leverages the proven instrumentation infrastructure that already exists in the codebase rather than building a new tracking system. It enables centralized handling of all metrics including tokens without disrupting other monitoring capabilities.
consequences: Requires modification of BenchmarkHook protocol and its implementations
New hook methods must be implemented consistently across different instrumentation points
Provides unified way to track performance alongside token usage in production environments
Allows for future extension if additional tracking dimensions are needed
alternatives_considered: Build new dedicated tracking infrastructure - rejected because it duplicates existing functionality and would increase maintenance burden
Modify provider interfaces directly to include token support - rejected because this changes core contracts that could break other implementations in the codebase
Use context managers at API layer - rejected because there's no way to access raw usage data from providers without modifying their return types

adrs.title: ADR: Add Token Usage Fields to Query Request and Response Models
adrs.context: API endpoints currently have no mechanism for including or returning token usage information in responses. Users cannot see how many tokens their queries consumed.
adrs.decision: Extend the API response models (QueryResponse, ChatResponse) with fields that include detailed token usage metrics from query processing sessions.
adrs.rationale: This provides a clear and direct way for users to access token costs of their operations through standard API responses. It aligns with REST conventions where request/response metadata is included in payloads.
consequences: API models must be updated to include new fields for usage data
All existing clients will need to handle potentially new optional fields gracefully
Increases response payload size slightly but provides valuable cost visibility
Requires coordination with frontend UI teams to display token usage appropriately
alternatives_considered: Add headers for token counts - rejected because it's not RESTful and makes the API less discoverable
Return usage in custom metadata fields - rejected because this complicates client integration unnecessarily
Only log usage rather than return it - rejected because users actively want to know query costs

components.name: ChatProvider
components.purpose: Protocol defining the interface for synchronous chat interactions with LLM providers
responsibilities: Handle synchronous chat requests to language models
Return string responses from LLM calls

components.name: StreamingChatProvider
components.purpose: Protocol defining the interface for streaming chat interactions with LLM providers
responsibilities: Handle streaming chat requests to language models
Return iterators of string responses from LLM calls

components.name: EmbeddingProvider
components.purpose: Protocol defining the interface for embedding text using LLM providers
responsibilities: Generate embeddings for single text inputs
Generate batch embeddings for multiple text inputs
Provide dimensionality information of embeddings

components.name: RerankProvider
components.purpose: Protocol defining the interface for document reranking using LLM providers
responsibilities: Rank documents based on query relevance
Return ranked results with scores
interfaces: rerank(query: str, documents: list[str], top_n: int | None) -> list[RerankResult]

components.name: VisionProvider
components.purpose: Protocol defining the interface for image description using LLM providers
responsibilities: Describe images provided as base64 encoded strings
Accept optional prompts for guidance
interfaces: describe_image(image_base64: str, prompt: str | None) -> str

components.name: FitzKragEngine
components.purpose: Main engine for knowledge routing augmented generation using KRAG approach
responsibilities: Load collections and manage components
Analyze queries and determine processing strategy
Execute query answering pipeline with retrieval and generation
Handle cloud caching of results
interfaces: _store_cloud_cache(query_text: str, addresses: list, answer: Answer) -> None
point(source: Path, collection: str | None) -> Any
_fast_index_code_files(manifest: Any, source_dir: Path, progress: Callable[[str], None] | None) -> None

components.name: fitz
components.purpose: Stateful SDK interface for the Fitz KRAG framework
responsibilities: Provide high-level interface to manage collections and query knowledge bases
Handle configuration management and initialization
interfaces: __init__(collection: str, config_path: Optional[Union[str, Path]], auto_init: bool) -> None
ask(question: str, top_k: Optional[int], conversation_context: Optional['ConversationContext']) -> Answer

components.name: BenchmarkHook
components.purpose: Protocol for benchmarking plugin performance with timing and call tracking
responsibilities: Track start of method calls with context information
Record end of method calls with results and errors
interfaces: on_call_start(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
on_call_end(context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None

components.name: CachingHook
components.purpose: Protocol for caching plugin results with get and store operations
responsibilities: Retrieve cached results from storage
Store computed results in cache
interfaces: get_cached_result(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
cache_result(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict, result: Any) -> None

components.name: InstrumentedProxy
components.purpose: Wrapper that instruments method calls with benchmarking hooks
responsibilities: Wrap target objects to intercept and instrument method calls
Track call timing, caching behavior, and other performance metrics
interfaces: __init__(target: Any, layer: str, plugin_name: str, methods_to_track: set[str] | None) -> None

integration_points: ExternalSystem — LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) that implement ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, RerankProvider, and VisionProvider protocols

interfaces: rerank(query: str, documents: list[str], top_n: int | None) -> list[RerankResult]

components.name: OllamaChat
components.purpose: Implementation of ChatProvider for Ollama services using direct HTTP calls
responsibilities: Handle synchronous chat requests to local Ollama models
Return string responses from Ollama API calls
interfaces: __init__(model: str | None, tier: ModelTier, base_url: str | None, models: dict[ModelTier, str] | None) -> None

components.name: OllamaEmbedding
components.purpose: Implementation of EmbeddingProvider for Ollama embedding services using direct HTTP calls
responsibilities: Generate embeddings for text inputs using local Ollama models
Support batch operations and dimension queries
interfaces: __init__(model: str | None, base_url: str | None, num_ctx: int | None) -> None

components.name: OllamaRerank
components.purpose: Implementation of RerankProvider for Ollama reranking services using direct HTTP calls
responsibilities: Rank documents based on query relevance using local Ollama models
Return ranked results with scores
interfaces: __init__(model: str | None, base_url: str | None) -> None
rerank(query: str, documents: list[str], top_n: int | None) -> list[RerankResult]

components.name: OllamaVision
components.purpose: Implementation of VisionProvider for Ollama vision services using direct HTTP calls
responsibilities: Describe images using local Ollama vision models
Accept optional prompts for guidance
interfaces: __init__(model: str | None, base_url: str | None) -> None
describe_image(image_base64: str, prompt: str | None) -> str

components.name: FitzKragEngine
components.purpose: Main engine for knowledge routing augmented generation using KRAG approach
responsibilities: Load collections and manage components
Analyze queries and determine processing strategy
Execute query answering pipeline with retrieval and generation
Handle cloud caching of results
interfaces: _store_cloud_cache(query_text: str, addresses: list, answer: Answer) -> None
point(source: Path, collection: str | None) -> Any
_fast_index_code_files(manifest: Any, source_dir: Path, progress: Callable[[str], None] | None) -> None

components.name: fitz
components.purpose: Stateful SDK interface for the Fitz KRAG framework
responsibilities: Provide high-level interface to manage collections and query knowledge bases
Handle configuration management and initialization
interfaces: __init__(collection: str, config_path: Optional[Union[str, Path]], auto_init: bool) -> None
ask(question: str, top_k: Optional[int], conversation_context: Optional['ConversationContext']) -> Answer

components.name: BenchmarkHook
components.purpose: Protocol for benchmarking plugin performance with timing and call tracking
responsibilities: Track start of method calls with context information
Record end of method calls with results and errors
interfaces: on_call_start(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
on_call_end(context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None

components.name: CachingHook
components.purpose: Protocol for caching plugin results with get and store operations
responsibilities: Retrieve cached results from storage
Store computed results in cache
interfaces: get_cached_result(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
cache_result(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict, result: Any) -> None

components.name: InstrumentedProxy
components.purpose: Wrapper that instruments method calls with benchmarking hooks
responsibilities: Wrap target objects to intercept and instrument method calls
Track call timing, caching behavior, and other performance metrics
interfaces: __init__(target: Any, layer: str, plugin_name: str, methods_to_track: set[str] | None) -> None

integration_points: ExternalSystem — LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) that implement ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, RerankProvider, and VisionProvider protocols

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
phases.verification_command: pytest fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_base.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_openai.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_cohere.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_ollama.py

phases.name: Create TokenTrackingHook Implementation
phases.objective: Implement a BenchmarkHook that counts and logs token usage for chat and embed methods using length-based estimation
deliverables: New file fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/token_hook.py with TokenTrackingHook class
estimate_tokens helper function using len(text) // 4 approximation
key_risks: Approximate token estimation may be inaccurate for multilingual or special character inputs
InstrumentedProxy wrapping may break method signatures or parameter passing
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.instrumentation.token_hook import TokenTrackingHook; print('Import successful')"

phases.name: Update LLM Factory to Register Hook with Providers
phases.objective: Modify LLM factory methods to register the new token tracking hook and wrap relevant provider instances
deliverables: Updated fitz_ai/llm/factory.py with TokenTrackingHook import and registration logic
key_risks: Providers created through complex composition or caching may not receive hook registration
Hook registration order dependency may cause incorrect tracking if other hooks modify state first
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.factory import get_chat_factory; print('Factory imports successfully')"

phases.name: Validate Instrumentation Integration with Engine & SDK
phases.objective: Ensure token counting works end-to-end through FitzKragEngine.answer() and fitz.ask() methods
deliverables: Test demonstrating logs showing input/output tokens per call in test environment
key_risks: Logging infrastructure not initialized may cause silent failures in test environments
Hook registration occurring too late after provider creation leads to inconsistent token reporting

risks.description: The InstrumentedProxy may fail to wrap provider methods correctly, causing token tracking to fail silently.
risks.mitigation: Implement comprehensive integration tests that verify proxy wrapping behavior and ensure method signatures are preserved during instrumentation.
risks.contingency: Fallback to manual logging within each provider class if InstrumentedProxy fails, with monitoring alerts for tracking disablement.
risks.verification: Run integration test that creates a provider and calls chat/embed to verify logs are generated

risks.description: Length-based text estimation may be significantly inaccurate for multilingual or special character inputs.
risks.mitigation: Implement unit tests comparing length estimates against known token counts on common English text samples to validate approximation accuracy.
risks.contingency: Add configurable tokenizer fallback mechanism that can be enabled in production environments where precision is critical.
risks.verification: Unit test that compares length estimate vs real token count for common English text samples

risks.description: Hook registration order dependency may cause inaccurate tracking due to incorrect hook execution sequence.
risks.mitigation: Ensure register_hook is called before any provider instantiation by centralizing the instrumentation setup in a configuration initialization step.
risks.contingency: Implement logging validation that tracks when hooks are registered and providers instantiated to detect order issues during testing.
risks.verification: Add test ensuring register_hook happens before any provider instantiation

risks.description: Memory leak potential in HTTP clients due to multiple proxy wrapper creation leading to resource accumulation or premature client closing.
risks.mitigation: Implement tests that repeatedly create providers and call methods under stress conditions while monitoring for resource leaks via memory profiling tools.
risks.contingency: Add explicit cleanup logic in provider classes' __del__ methods to ensure proper disposal of wrapped httpx clients, with warning logs if cleanup fails.
risks.verification: Instrumentation test that repeatedly creates providers and calls methods

risks.description: LLM Provider API changes could break token tracking wrappers if method signatures evolve unexpectedly.
risks.mitigation: Validate against known provider files that match current patterns (openai.py, anthropic.py, etc.) to ensure compatibility with existing contract definitions.
risks.contingency: Implement defensive programming in hooks using getattr and try/except blocks around method access points to gracefully handle signature mismatches.
risks.verification: Validate against known provider files that match current patterns

risks.description: Logging infrastructure not initialized during unit tests will cause silent failure of token tracking logs.
risks.mitigation: Ensure all test environments initialize logging correctly and add monitoring for missing log entries that would indicate tracking disablement.
risks.contingency: Implement error reporting within the hook when it detects uninitialized logger or empty log handlers, triggering alerts during CI runs.
risks.verification: Test that hook logs appear in structured format under normal conditions

risks.description: Hook registration occurring too late after provider instantiation will result in inconsistent token reporting.
risks.mitigation: Design the system with explicit initialization order control, ensuring instrumentation setup occurs before any LLM factory invocations.
risks.contingency: Add lifecycle validation tests showing full sequence: register hook → create provider → call methods, and implement assertion checks that confirm tracking works end-to-end.
risks.verification: Add a test that shows full lifecycle: register hook → create provider → call methods

recommended_contingencies: Implement fallback manual logging within each provider class if InstrumentedProxy fails
Add configurable tokenizer fallback mechanism for production environments requiring precision
Implement explicit cleanup logic in provider classes' __del__ methods to ensure proper disposal of wrapped httpx clients
Use defensive programming in hooks with getattr and try/except blocks around method access points
